title: Valid registration succeeds with proper payload
steps: Prepare JSON payload with email 'user@example.com', password 'SecurePass123!', mobile '+1234567890', firstName 'John', lastName 'Doe'. | Send POST request to '/api/v1/users/register' with Content-Type 'application/json'. | Receive response and verify status 201 Created; response body contains 'userId' and 'authToken'; confirm that a verification email is sent.
expected: Response status 201 Created with payload containing userId and authToken; email verification is dispatched.

title: Registration fails when email is already registered
steps: Prepare payload with email 'existing@example.com' and valid other fields. | Send POST request to '/api/v1/users/register' with JSON body. | Verify response status 409 Conflict and that the error message equals 'Email already registered'.
expected: Response status 409 Conflict with error message 'Email already registered'.

title: Password validation failures return specific errors
steps: Prepare payload with email 'user3@example.com', password 'password', and valid mobile/formatted as '+15551234567', firstName 'Alice', lastName 'Smith'. | Send POST request to '/api/v1/users/register' with JSON body. | Verify response status 400 Bad Request and that the response includes error 'Validation failed' with details listing password validation errors.
expected: Response status 400 Bad Request with error 'Validation failed' and details: [ 'Password must contain at least 1 uppercase letter', 'Password must contain at least 1 number', 'Password must contain at least 1 special character' ].

title: Email format validation rejects invalid email
steps: Prepare payload with invalid email 'not-an-email' and valid other fields. | Send POST request to '/api/v1/users/register' with JSON body. | Verify response status 400 Bad Request with error 'Invalid email format' or details containing 'Invalid email format'.
expected: Response status 400 Bad Request with error message 'Invalid email format'.

title: Rate limiting per IP limits registration attempts to 5 per hour
steps: From the same IP address, send 5 consecutive registration requests using a valid payload. | Send a 6th registration request from the same IP. | Verify response status 429 Too Many Requests with an appropriate error message indicating rate limit exceeded.
expected: Response status 429 Too Many Requests after 5 successful attempts with error indicating rate limit exceeded.
